children.heading: Injunctions
children.label: a Injunctions
children.text: Whenever the Attorney General of the United States has reasonable cause to believe that a person is engaged in a violation of this chapter, the Attorney General may bring a civil action in the appropriate district court of the United States requesting such relief, including a permanent or temporary injunction, restraining order, or other order, against the person, as the Attorney General determines to be necessary to restrain the person from continuing to engage in, sanction, promote, or otherwise participate in a professional boxing match in violation of this chapter.

children.heading: Managers, promoters, matchmakers, and licensees
children.label: 1 Managers, promoters, matchmakers, and licensees
children.text: Any manager, promoter, matchmaker, and licensee who knowingly violates, or coerces or causes any other person to violate, any provision of this chapter, other than section 6307a(b), 6307b, 6307c, 6307d, 6307e, 6307f, or 6307h of this title, shall, upon conviction, be imprisoned for not more than 1 year or fined not more than $20,000, or both.1So in original. Section 6307a does not contain a subsec. (b).1

children.heading: Violation of antiexploitation, sanctioning organization, or disclosure provisions
children.label: 2 Violation of antiexploitation, sanctioning organization, or disclosure provisions
B
children.text: if a violation occurs in connection with a professional boxing match the gross revenues for which exceed $2,000,000, an additional amount which bears the same ratio to $100,000 as the amount of such revenues compared to $2,000,000, or both.

children.heading: Conflict of interest
children.label: 3 Conflict of interest
children.text: Any member or employee of a boxing commission, any person who administers or enforces State boxing laws, and any member of the Association of Boxing Commissions who knowingly violates shall, upon conviction, be imprisoned for not more than 1 year or fined not more than $20,000, or both.section 6308(a) of this title

children.heading: Boxers
children.label: 4 Boxers
children.text: Any boxer who knowingly violates any provision of this chapter shall, upon conviction, be fined not more than $1,000.
to enjoin the holding of any professional boxing match which the practice involves;
to enforce compliance with this chapter;
to obtain the fines provided under subsection (b) or appropriate restitution; or
to obtain such other relief as the court may deem appropriate.

children.heading: Private right of action
children.label: d Private right of action
children.text: Any boxer who suffers economic injury as a result of a violation of any provision of this chapter may bring an action in the appropriate Federal or State court and recover the damages suffered, court costs, and reasonable attorneys fees and expenses.

children.heading: Enforcement against Federal Trade Commission, State Attorneys General, etc.
children.label: e Enforcement against Federal Trade Commission, State Attorneys General, etc.

children.text: any provision of this chapter against the Federal Trade Commission, the United States Attorney General, or the chief legal officer of any State for acting or failing to act in an official capacity;
subsection (d) of this section against a State or political subdivision of a State, or any agency or instrumentality thereof; or
against a boxer acting in his capacity as a boxer.section 6307b of this title
